name: Frequency Counting
description: Questions on counting elements with dict or Counter.

questions.question: collections.Counter is best used for:
questions.explanation: Counter counts occurrences of hashable elements and provides most_common(), etc.

questions.question: To count character frequencies in a string s, a common pattern is:
questions.explanation: Counter(s) counts each character (or element in any iterable).

questions.question: Counter('aab').most_common(1) returns:
questions.explanation: most_common(1) returns the single most common element and its count as a list of tuples.

questions.question: Two strings are anagrams if their character counts:
questions.explanation: Anagrams have the same character frequencies; comparing Counter(s1) == Counter(s2) works.

questions.question: Building a frequency dict with a loop: d.get(x, 0) + 1 is used to:
questions.explanation: get(x,0)+1 increments the count for x, adding the key with count 1 if missing.
